steps.title: Trace Laidre's reasoning
steps.content: Laidre disagrees that the tusk has such an important purpose. Her counter-argument: if the tusk were really that important to survival, evolution would make it widespread — but in fact many narwhals don't have one.

steps.title: Complete the inference
steps.content:
The natural completion is (A) "more narwhals would have a tusk" — that's the prediction her reasoning yields, and the fact that they don't is what undercuts the claim.

(B), (C), and (D) don't connect to the importance-of-the-tusk argument.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
